text:
Quoted in Al Jazeera today. It's not about crude, it's about refined products.

"Diesel, in particular, is grappling both from the loss of supply from the Middle East refineries, and from Russian refineries facing relentless attacks by Ukrainian drones, leading to skyrocketing diesel prices beyond seasonal norms against crude."

#oott

https://t.co/HygJyMinMl

CHART OF THE DAY: Global oil stocks posted an increase in June (entirely due to rising volumes of oil on water), according to @IEA estimates.  It's the first monthly increase since the war, and comes after stocks fell ~360 million barres (or ~3.9m b/d) from March through May. https://t.co/uoH0D6kLfT

BOOM: @IEA says UAE lifted its crude production to a **record high** of 4.1 million barrels a day in June (including condensates and natural gas liquids, total oil liquids output soared to an impressive 4.8m b/d).

U.S. gasoline consumption and fuel economy

Global petroleum consumption was permanently lowered by the long-term impact of the oil shocks in the 1970s and again between 2008 and 2014, partly as a result of switching to other fuels, but mainly because of efficiency improvements.

The sharp rise in prices made petroleum too expensive to use for electricity generation as well as for heating homes, offices and public buildings, and led to it being phased out in favour of other fuels.

Even in the transport sector, however, consumption was pushed onto a lower trajectory as motorists and policymakers focused on greater fuel economy to cut costs and reduce reliance on imports.

In the United States, the impact of higher prices and greater fuel economy has cut gasoline consumption by more than one-third compared with its pre-shock trend ...

China had 1.7 billion bbls for crude storage, not 1.3 billion bbls. 

Yeesh.

(MEMO) Memo - Insane Asylum

The Strait of Hormuz is not back to normal, so why is everyone assuming the oil market is going back to the way it was?

Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
Days of strikes by the two countries have given way to an uneasy pause as Qatari mediators seek to salvage the tattered U.S.-Iran truce.

Delta Air Lines Inc. reaffirmed its full-year profit guidance and said strong demand for premium, corporate and international travel helped offset the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history.

The Atlanta-based airline earned an adjusted $1.56 a share in the second quarter, it said on Friday, topping analysts’ estimates of $1.51. Revenue rose 14% from a year earlier, with capacity increasing just 1%.
#oott https://t.co/kQKxLRieJq

Reuters: "Russian gasoline output fell to a level equivalent to only around 65% of the ​seasonal average consumption after Ukrainian drone attacks led to stoppages at large oil refineries, according to two industry sources ‌and Reuters calculations." https://t.co/2i4jRgof02
UAE OIL OUTPUT HIT RECORD HIGH OF 4.1M B/D IN JUNE, IEA SAYS
🚨 Analyst alleges that the IEA is "double counting" UAE oil production https://t.co/ttfUdnPU8o
India’s state-owned Oil and Natural Gas Corp. approved an expansion of the country’s strategic oil reserves, highlighting a push to strengthen energy resilience following the shock of the Iran war.

Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has slowed to a near-standstill as shipping risks escalated, with ship-tracking data showing only two vessels transiting the strait on Thursday morning. Escalating military strikes between the US and Iran have prompted marine insurers to advise companies to pause their voyages in the Gulf.

IEA: Global observed oil inventories rose for the first time in four months in June, by 21 mb, as sharply higher oil on water volumes more than offset continued draws in onshore tanks
